places.name: Hawa Mahal
places.description: The Palace of Winds is a five-storey honeycomb façade built in 1799 for royal ladies to observe street life while remaining unseen. Intricate latticework and pink sandstone make it an iconic symbol of Jaipur.
places.weather: Cool and dry; clear skies ideal for photography

nearestAttractions.name: City Palace
nearestAttractions.description: Royal complex with museums, courtyards and the Mubarak Mahal — rich in Rajasthani and Mughal architecture.

nearestAttractions.name: Jantar Mantar
nearestAttractions.description: An 18th-century astronomical observatory with large stone instruments; UNESCO site.

places.name: Amber Fort
places.description: A hilltop fort overlooking Maota Lake, known for its artistic Hindu style architecture, mirrored Sheesh Mahal, and panoramic views over Jaipur.
places.weather: Cool mornings; pleasant afternoons for exploration

nearestAttractions.name: Jaigarh Fort
nearestAttractions.description: Fortress known for its cannon collection and robust ramparts, set on the Aravalli hills.

nearestAttractions.name: Panna Meena Ka Kund
nearestAttractions.description: A historic stepwell with geometric steps — popular photography spot near Amber Fort.

places.name: Lake Pichola
places.description: A scenic artificial lake surrounded by palaces, ghats and islands; famous for sunset boat rides and postcard views of the City Palace.
places.weather: Cool and breezy; evenings ideal for boat trips

nearestAttractions.name: City Palace (Udaipur)
nearestAttractions.description: A large complex of palaces and museums overlooking Lake Pichola featuring intricate balconies and courtyards.

nearestAttractions.name: Jag Mandir
nearestAttractions.description: Island palace on Lake Pichola, often used for leisure boat stops and scenic photos.

places.name: City Palace (Udaipur)
places.description: An architectural marvel made of marble and granite, housing museums with royal artifacts and offering sweeping lake views.

nearestAttractions.name: Bagore Ki Haveli
nearestAttractions.description: Converted haveli hosting cultural shows, traditional costumes and folk performances.

nearestAttractions.name: Fateh Sagar Lake
nearestAttractions.description: A nearby placid lake with boating and promenade space, great for morning walks.

places.name: India Gate
places.description: A war memorial honoring Indian soldiers, set along a grand ceremonial boulevard. Evenings are lively with locals and food stalls around the lawns.
places.weather: Cool and comfortable; clear skies at night

nearestAttractions.name: Rajpath & Rashtrapati Bhavan
nearestAttractions.description: The ceremonial axis of New Delhi with government buildings and the Presidential residence.

nearestAttractions.name: National War Memorial
nearestAttractions.description: Contemporary memorial honoring the fallen soldiers with a reflective layout.

places.name: Qutub Minar
places.description: A 73-metre tall minaret, a UNESCO World Heritage site, known for intricate Indo-Islamic architecture and surrounding archaeological ruins.

nearestAttractions.name: Iron Pillar
nearestAttractions.description: Ancient rust-resistant pillar located within the Qutub complex, famed for metallurgy.

nearestAttractions.name: Alai Darwaza
nearestAttractions.description: An impressive gateway demonstrating early Indo-Islamic architecture.

places.name: Jama Masjid
places.description: One of India’s largest mosques, built by Shah Jahan; offers amazing city views from its minarets and a bustling market (Chandni Chowk) nearby.
places.weather: Cool mornings; crowded afternoons during festivals

nearestAttractions.name: Chandni Chowk
nearestAttractions.description: Historic market street famous for street food, narrow lanes and traditional bazaars.

nearestAttractions.name: Red Fort
nearestAttractions.description: Massive Mughal fort and UNESCO site hosting evening sound-and-light shows.

places.name: Gateway of India
places.description: Built in 1924, the Gateway overlooks the Arabian Sea and is a ceremonial entrance to Mumbai — popular for ferry rides to Elephanta and sunset views.
places.weather: Pleasant with sea breeze; humid in monsoon

nearestAttractions.name: Elephanta Caves
nearestAttractions.description: Island cave temples with rock-cut sculptures dedicated to Shiva; accessible by ferry.

nearestAttractions.name: Colaba Causeway
nearestAttractions.description: Vibrant street for shopping, cafes and antiques close to Gateway Area.

places.name: Marine Drive
places.description: Popular evening promenade called the Queen’s Necklace; great for sunset walks and skyline views of South Mumbai.
places.weather: Mild and breezy; humid in monsoon

nearestAttractions.name: Chowpatty Beach
nearestAttractions.description: Adjacent public beach famous for local snacks and lively crowds at sundown.

nearestAttractions.name: Nariman Point
nearestAttractions.description: Prime business district and skyline viewpoint at the southern tip of Marine Drive.

places.name: Sinhagad Fort
places.description: Historic hill fort with trekking routes, panoramic views of the Deccan plateau and famous local snacks at the top.
places.weather: Cool and breezy; lush during monsoon

nearestAttractions.name: Kondana Caves
nearestAttractions.description: Ancient Buddhist caves near the fort, offering history and scenic treks.

nearestAttractions.name: Khambatki Ghat
nearestAttractions.description: Scenic mountain pass with viewpoints near Sinhagad.

places.name: Shaniwar Wada
places.description: Once the seat of the Peshwas, an important Maratha-era monument with ruins and evocative history.

nearestAttractions.name: Raja Dinkar Kelkar Museum
nearestAttractions.description: Museum showcasing Indian decorative arts, musical instruments and daily life artifacts.

nearestAttractions.name: Pune University Campus
nearestAttractions.description: Lush campus area with architectural and cultural significance.

places.name: Lalbagh Botanical Garden
places.description: A historic 240-acre botanical garden with a famous glasshouse, seasonal flower shows and rare plant species.

nearestAttractions.name: Bangalore Palace
nearestAttractions.description: A Tudor-style palace with lavish interiors and occasional cultural events.

nearestAttractions.name: Cubbon Park
nearestAttractions.description: Large public park near the city center with shady trails and colonial-era buildings.

places.name: Bangalore Palace
places.description: Inspired by Windsor Castle, featuring woodcarvings, stained glass and sprawling grounds used for concerts and events.

nearestAttractions.name: Tipu Sultan's Summer Palace
nearestAttractions.description: Historic teak structure and museum dedicated to Tipu Sultan.

nearestAttractions.name: ISKCON Temple, Bangalore
nearestAttractions.description: Modern temple complex hosting devotional cultural events and vegetarian dining.

places.name: Mysore Palace
places.description: A glorious Indo-Saracenic palace famed for Dussehra celebrations and nightly illuminations in thousands of bulbs.
places.weather: Pleasant with festive vibes in Dussehra

nearestAttractions.name: Chamundi Hill
nearestAttractions.description: Sacred hill with temple and sweeping city views, near the palace area.

nearestAttractions.name: Brindavan Gardens
nearestAttractions.description: Famous terraced gardens with musical fountains next to the dam.

places.name: The Ridge (Shimla)
places.description: A wide open pedestrian area in Shimla offering panoramic views of the Himalayas, colonial architecture and seasonal festivals.
places.bestTimeToVisit: March to June; December for snow
places.weather: Cool summers; cold and snowy in peak winter

nearestAttractions.name: Mall Road
nearestAttractions.description: Bustling street with shops, cafes and colonial charm; perfect for evening strolls.

nearestAttractions.name: Jakhu Temple
nearestAttractions.description: Ancient hilltop temple dedicated to Lord Hanuman with city views and playful monkeys.

places.name: Kufri
places.description: Small resort town famous for hiking, pony rides and winter snow activities; panoramic Himalayan viewpoints.
places.bestTimeToVisit: March to June; December to February (snow)

nearestAttractions.name: Mahasu Peak
nearestAttractions.description: The highest point around Kufri offering breathtaking Himalayan views and trekking.

nearestAttractions.name: Fagu
nearestAttractions.description: Quiet village with apple orchards and scenic walks, close to Kufri.

places.name: Solang Valley
places.description: Adventure hub near Manali offering paragliding, zorbing, skiing in winter and picturesque alpine meadows.
places.bestTimeToVisit: May to October for adventure; December to February for skiing
places.weather: Cool mountain climate; heavy snow in winter

nearestAttractions.name: Atal Tunnel (Rohtang)
nearestAttractions.description: Record-breaking high-altitude highway tunnel providing access to Lahaul-Spiti and shorter routes to Manali.

nearestAttractions.name: Beas River
nearestAttractions.description: Scenic river with riverside walks and rafting spots near Manali.

places.name: Old Manali
places.description: Laid-back part of Manali with cafes, artisan shops, and a backpacker vibe — great for easing into mountain life.
places.bestTimeToVisit: March to June; September to November
places.weather: Cool and pleasant outside winter months

nearestAttractions.name: Vashisht Hot Water Springs
nearestAttractions.description: Natural hot springs near Old Manali, believed to have therapeutic properties.

nearestAttractions.name: Hadimba Devi Temple
nearestAttractions.description: Ancient wooden temple set in cedar forests near Manali.
